steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined phrase: “particularly at a time when the manufacturing of new television sets was stalled due to the US’s involvement in World War II.” This phrase immediately follows the claim that advertisers “hesitated to jump into a new space.” The key task is determining what role the phrase plays relative to that hesitance.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It describes how broadcasters attempted to convince advertisers to support television.: The underlined phrase says nothing about broadcasters’ persuasion tactics; that topic appears in the next sentence, not here. ✗
  • Choice B — It explains why a type of television programming was popular at the time.: The phrase does not mention programming popularity at all; it concerns TV set manufacturing being stalled. ✗
  • Choice C — It identifies a specific reason behind some advertisers' hesitance to support television.: The phrase uses “particularly” to sharpen the reason for advertisers’ hesitance: TV set production was halted during WWII, so there was no growing audience to advertise to. This is precisely a specific reason for the hesitance named in the preceding clause. ✓
  • Choice D — It compares the beginnings of radio programming with the beginnings of television programming in the United States.: The phrase mentions neither radio nor a comparison; radio is referenced earlier in the passage but not in the underlined phrase. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
